---
title: Domestic migration patterns shift toward Rural areas
description: Line chart showing domestic net migration trends for rural and nonrural
  areas since the Great Recession.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-net-migration-since-gr-lc-domestic-only/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Demographics
  - Migration
  - Rural Development
tags:
  - IRS-SOI
  - Migration
  - Domestic Migration
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Line Chart
image: 3.4_net_migration_since_GR_lc_domestic_only.svg
chart:
  title: Domestic migration patterns shift toward Rural areas
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Internal Revenue Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.irs.gov/statistics/soi-tax-stats-migration-data
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - irs-migration
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart tracks domestic (non-international) migration patterns, showing a notable shift toward rural areas beginning around 2020.](./3.4_net_migration_since_GR_lc_domestic_only.svg)

## Overview

This chart tracks domestic (non-international) migration patterns, showing a notable shift toward rural areas beginning around 2020.

## Key Findings

- Domestic migration to rural areas turned positive around 2020
- The pandemic accelerated a reversal in longstanding urban migration trends
- Rural areas are now net gainers of domestic migrants

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/net_migration_lc_domestic_only.R` in the producing project.
